flashcards.front: What is the JDBC type mapping for java.sql.Types.VARCHAR?
flashcards.back: Maps to Java String type. Used with getString() and setString() methods in JDBC.

flashcards.front: Which JDBC method is used to retrieve INTEGER values from a ResultSet?
flashcards.back: getInt() method - retrieves the value as a Java int. Returns 0 for NULL values unless checked with wasNull().

flashcards.front: What SQL type corresponds to java.sql.Date?
flashcards.back: DATE - stores year, month, day only (no time component). Use setDate() and getDate() methods.

flashcards.front: How do you handle NULL values for primitive types in JDBC?
flashcards.back: Use the primitive's wrapper class (Integer, Double, etc.) with getObject() or check wasNull() after retrieval to avoid default values.

flashcards.front: What JDBC type is used for storing large text data (CLOB)?
flashcards.back: java.sql.Clob interface - use setCharacterStream() to set and getString() or getCharacterStream() to retrieve.

flashcards.front: Which JDBC type maps to Java boolean?
flashcards.back: BIT or BOOLEAN (depending on database) - use getBoolean() and setBoolean() methods.

flashcards.front: What is the mapping for BLOB in JDBC?
flashcards.back: Binary Large Object - maps to Java byte[] using getBytes() or the java.sql.Blob interface for streaming.
Maps to SQL TIMESTAMP (date + time + nanoseconds). Use setTimestamp() and getTimestamp() with java.sql.Timestamp class.

flashcards.front: What method retrieves a FLOAT/DOUBLE from ResultSet?
flashcards.back: getDouble() - returns Java double. For precise decimal values, prefer getBigDecimal() to avoid floating-point errors.

flashcards.front: What is the generic method for retrieving any data type in JDBC?
flashcards.back: getObject() - returns java.lang.Object. For numeric types, returns Integer, Double, etc.; for strings, returns String; respects NULL.

flashcards.front: Which JDBC type maps to Java long (int64)?
flashcards.back: BIGINT - use getLong() and setLong() methods. Suitable for storing large integer values beyond int range.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between getDate() and getTimestamp()?
flashcards.back: getDate() returns only date portion (year, month, day). getTimestamp() returns date + time + nanoseconds in a Timestamp object.
